summary:
I'll build the Three Rivers Cooperative Credit Union site. Let me first survey the working directory, then build out the stylesheet, pages, and supporting files.
Now I'll build the home page (almanac cover):
I'll continue building the remaining pages directly — the work is straightforward sequential HTML generation that doesn't benefit from task tracking.
Now the site_intent.json describing the concept as implemented:
All seven HTML pages plus the shared stylesheet, JavaScript, and site_intent.json are in place.

## Summary

Built **Three Rivers Cooperative Credit Union** — an editorial-almanac website for a 90-year-old cooperative bank. The design leans into the warm institutional brief: olive-green ledger-stripe banding divides each page into "chapters" announced by terracotta Roman numerals, with monospaced figures for rates, a rounded humanist serif for headlines, scratchboard-style inline-SVG engravings (bridge, wheat, river-mark, board portraits, tool icons), and the seven Rochdale Principles in tiny caps in every footer.

**Interactive features implemented in vanilla JS:**
- Six fully-working calculators (mortgage P&I+T+I+PMI, auto+refi savings, debt consolidation, savings projector, HELOC, 12-month amortization table) with live recalculation and print-to-PDF
- Live countdown to the June 14, 2026 annual meeting (on home + governance)
- Sticky outline-rail scrollspy on the services index
- Tabbed UIs on Find Us (branches/ATM heatmap/filters) and Sign In (personal/business)
- Expandable branch listings grouped by river basin with archive notes
- Scroll-snap past-issues carousel with prev/next controls
- Fraud-alert subscription toggles with live state
- Mobile marginalia tap-to-expand glosses

**Files written (10 total):**
- `index.html` — almanac cover home with 4 chapters + member voices
- `services.html` — tiered services index with sticky rail
- `calculators.html` — six-tool workshop
- `find-us.html` — regional SVG map + basin-grouped branches
- `governance.html` — RSVP, board gallery, ballot panel, annual-report spine grid, Rochdale longform
- `reading-room.html` — Issue No. 109 broadsheet periodical with Loans-at-Work column
- `sign-in.html` — two-column auth with engraved trust marks + fraud hotline
- `css/style.css` — shared stylesheet (~900 lines, custom properties, full responsive collapse rules)
- `js/app.js` — vanilla interactions (~270 lines)
- `site_intent.json` — concept-as-implemented manifest
All seven HTML pages plus the shared stylesheet, JavaScript, and site_intent.json are in place.
